title: External npm packages not found in code node
symptom: When using the code node to require external npm packages, an error indicates that the package is not found. This occurs in self-hosted n8n deployments without proper package installation.
applies_when: Trying to require external npm packages in a code node
not_this_if: Using a Docker-based n8n deployment with packages installed
root_cause: In self-hosted n8n deployments, external npm packages are not available by default. They need to be installed in the n8n Docker image and configured to be accessible in the code node through environment variables.
fix_steps: Create a Dockerfile that installs the required npm packages globally.
Set the NODE_PATH environment variable to include the global node_modules directory.
Update the docker-compose.yml to allow the external modules using NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_EXTERNAL.
Rebuild the Docker image and restart the n8n service.
agent_instruction: Follow the provided Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml instructions to install and configure external npm packages.
